response.summary: The implemented user-facing experience is not semantically fit for SL-003. The actual app entry surface still exposes internal factory/setup language instead of the item detail flow for marking an item done or setting a reminder, so the required heading, action wording, confirmation/status context, and user-value framing for this slice are not delivered to users. There are also weaker copy issues in planned-state labeling and no-next-step fallback messaging.

findings.observed: The rendered screen shows 'Fabric app factory' and 'App shell ready', which are internal/generic labels and do not tell the user they can complete a health item or set a reminder.
